title: Java Casting Overflow Math
description: Practice numeric casts, overflow, and exact arithmetic helpers.
learningGoals: Recall that narrowing casts keep low-order bits and can wrap values.
Recognize that normal int arithmetic overflows silently while Math.addExact throws.

completionChecks.prompt: What does Integer.MAX_VALUE + 1 evaluate to here?
choices: A long value, because the result is automatically widened
Integer.MIN_VALUE, because int addition silently wraps on overflow
An ArithmeticException, because Java checks every int overflow
A compile error, because the sum exceeds the int range
completionChecks.explanation: Plain int arithmetic wraps around on overflow, so MAX_VALUE + 1 becomes MIN_VALUE; Math.addExact is what throws ArithmeticException instead of wrapping.

completionChecks.prompt: What does System.out.println(wrapped) print after byte wrapped = (byte) 200;?
choices: Compilation fails because 200 does not fit in a byte
completionChecks.explanation: The explicit cast keeps the low 8 bits, so 200 wraps into the signed byte range as 200 - 256 = -56; the cast also suppresses the narrowing error the assignment would otherwise need.

completionChecks.prompt: What does the third System.out.println(widened + exact); print?
completionChecks.explanation: (byte) 200 wraps to -56 before widening to long, and -56 plus the exact result 15 prints -41.
